title: File organisation and access
summary: 9618 A Level file organisation — sequential, random, indexed-sequential access methods.
learningObjectives: Compare sequential and random file organisation.
Evaluate access methods for given scenarios.
Relate file design to processing requirements.

simpleExplanation.title: How records are stored and found
simpleExplanation.summary: File organisation trades storage efficiency against speed of access for different processing patterns.
steps: Sequential: records in order, batch processing.
Random/direct: hash or key maps to location.
Indexed-sequential: index speeds lookup in ordered file.
Choose method based on read/write frequency.

sections.content: **File organisation** (13.2) extends **13.1 file concepts**. Match **access method** to **application scenario**.
